market_summary: U.S. equities closed the week with a fourth straight weekly decline as oil-driven inflation risk and higher yields pressured risk assets. The S&P 500 finished March 20 at 6,506.48 and is down 5.0% year to date, while the Nasdaq is down 6.9% year to date. Volatility remains elevated in a mid-20s VIX environment after an early-March spike near 29.5.
key_drivers: Oil shock and conflict risk repriced inflation expectations, with Brent at 112.19 and WTI at 98.32 on March 20.
The Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% on March 18 and uncertainty remained elevated, keeping policy restrictive relative to growth expectations.
Treasury yields rose sharply, with the 10-year at 4.38%, tightening financial conditions for equity multiples.
Markets reduced expectations for 2026 rate cuts, removing a key support for long-duration growth stocks.
forward_outlook: Primary watch items are (1) whether VIX can hold below the recent high-volatility zone, (2) whether oil stabilizes below the latest spike range, and (3) whether yields stop repricing higher ahead of the next FOMC meeting on April 28-29, 2026. A de-escalation headline could trigger a sharp relief rally, but persistent energy stress keeps downside risk active.
lessons: In the current risk-off tape, preserve optionality first: smaller size, faster profit-taking, and strict drawdown limits should dominate decision-making.
